title: Raising awareness of esophageal cancer by expressing support for the designation of April 2025 as "Esophageal Cancer Awareness Month".
plain_summary: This bill wants to make April 2025 a special month to teach people about esophageal cancer. Esophageal cancer is a very dangerous type of cancer that grows fast and kills many people. It kills one American every thirty-six minutes, which means it is very deadly. The cancer is often caused by heartburn that lasts a long time, but doctors can find it early and treat it better if people get checked. The bill wants more people to learn about this cancer so they can protect themselves and get help sooner.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.

sections.title: Resolution Title
sections.text: This resolution supports making April 2025 Esophageal Cancer Awareness Month to help people learn about this deadly disease.

sections.title: Facts About Esophageal Cancer
sections.text: Esophageal cancer is growing faster than any other cancer in American men. It has increased by more than seven hundred percent in recent decades and is now one of the fastest growing cancers among all Americans. This cancer kills one American every thirty-six minutes every day. It is one of the deadliest cancers because only about one in five patients live for five years after being diagnosed. The number of younger Americans getting this cancer has tripled in recent decades. Most people with esophageal cancer are not diagnosed until the disease has spread, which makes treatment much harder. In the United States, this cancer is usually caused by ongoing heartburn or a condition called gastroesophageal reflux disease, also known as GERD. Many people who have GERD do not know it can be dangerous when it lasts for several weeks or months. Esophageal cancer can be a silent killer because patients often do not realize that heartburn, coughing, a hoarse voice, sore throat, chest pain, or other symptoms could be signs of GERD. People should talk to their doctor about screening for esophageal cancer if they have these symptoms. When stomach acid splashes into the esophagus repeatedly, it can cause cells to change and create a condition called Barrett's esophagus. This condition can increase a person's risk of getting esophageal cancer by thirty times. Teaching people about esophageal cancer helps them get preventive care, recognize symptoms, and find the disease early. Survivors, caregivers, doctors, and researchers have made great progress in creating better treatments and improving life for people with this disease. Making April Esophageal Cancer Awareness Month will encourage public education, support research, and promote early screening and prevention. This cancer can be prevented by finding Barrett's esophagus early, which doctors can treat and cure with outpatient procedures. Research shows that patients diagnosed with early-stage esophageal cancer have a much higher five-year survival rate of up to forty-nine percent compared to those diagnosed when the cancer has spread. This proves how important it is to screen for this cancer early and make people more aware of it.

sections.title: What the House of Representatives Resolves
sections.text: The House of Representatives supports making April Esophageal Cancer Awareness Month. The House encourages the federal government, states, local governments, nonprofit organizations, and the media to promote awareness of esophageal cancer. The House recognizes that we need more funding for research, better treatment options, and new ways to detect this cancer early. The House encourages people to learn about the risk factors and symptoms of esophageal cancer and to talk with their doctors about screening options. The House honors the courage and strength of people affected by esophageal cancer and supports their families and caregivers.
